unit.name: RNN mechanics: hidden state, unrolling, and vanilla equations
unit.description: Defines recurrent hidden state, unrolled RNN diagrams, output projection, and the vanilla tanh RNN.
unit.summary: An RNN has an input sequence x, output sequence y, and a recurrent internal hidden state that updates while the sequence is processed [ts=448s] [ts=470s]. The current hidden state depends on both x_t and h_{t-1}; unrolling makes this dependency and the gradient order explicit [ts=479s] [ts=501s]. The hidden-state recurrence applies the same function and same parameters W at every time step [ts=533s] [ts=571s]. A separate output function, often using W_hy, maps the hidden dimension to the output dimension and learns a transformation [ts=591s] [ts=618s]. Vanilla RNNs commonly use tanh for h_t because it is bounded, zero-centered, and can represent positive and negative values; the simplest output is a matrix multiply from h_t to y_t [ts=792s] [ts=823s].

key_points.text: RNNs maintain a hidden state updated as each input arrives.
Unrolling shows h_t depends on x_t and h_{t-1}.
The same recurrent function and parameters W are reused at every time step.
W_hy converts hidden state to output dimension through a learned projection.
Vanilla RNNs often use tanh because it is bounded and zero-centered.

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent neural networks
kp_catalog.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context. Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent neural networks formalizes how shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Vanilla RNN equations
kp_catalog.description: The tanh-based recurrent update and output projection used as the baseline RNN formulation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because vanilla RNN equations formalizes how the tanh-based recurrent update and output projection used as the baseline RNN formulation, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
